## What Happened

Prevented validation against inadequate sample size (n=48) or abandonment of external validity testing. Unlocked robust validation with 100× more data points.

## The Mechanism

When encountering paywalled Ad Fontes external validity data (n=48), the assistant triggered a gap-detection checkpoint: 'which sources are actually accessible and sufficient?' Rather than accepting the limitation, it proactively identified idiap/News-Media-Reliability (n=4,497 domains) — 100× larger coverage.

## Why It Matters

When external datasets are constrained by availability, treating that as a cognitive checkpoint (not a roadblock) unlocks better alternatives. Choosing datasets by statistical power rather than prestige measurably improves epistemic quality of validation.

## Counterfactual

Without this trigger: Proceed with paywalled Ad Fontes (low statistical power) or defer external validity validation entirely.

[broader pattern — other situations this applies to]
